title: QUIC Hosted Interop-Runner Handshake Corroboration Work Item
summary: Add a manual GitHub Actions workflow that runs the existing interop helper for one server-role handshake cell and uploads the artifact bundle for hosted review.
planned_changes:
- Add a `workflow_dispatch` GitHub Actions workflow for the narrow server-role `handshake` helper cell against a quic-go client slot.
- Check out this repository and `quic-interop-runner` as separate workspaces, then pass the explicit runner path to the existing helper.
- Upload the full `artifacts/interop-runner/` bundle with `if: always()` so success, advisory, and failure outcomes all preserve evidence.
- Document the hosted lane in the interop helper README and keep it out of ordinary push, pull-request, build, test, and package gates.
- Keep the helper's advisory classification and preserved runner report semantics unchanged.
out_of_scope: broad peer or role matrix expansion
making interop corroboration blocking in ordinary CI
verification_plan: Run the existing helper locally in dry-run mode and real server-role handshake mode against the local `C:\src\quic-interop\quic-interop-runner` checkout, then validate the SpecTrace JSON/profile surfaces. Hosted dispatch evidence remains a follow-up external artifact rather than a prerequisite for this repository configuration slice.
completion_notes: Implemented the manual hosted workflow at `.github/workflows/interop-runner-handshake.yml`, documented the lane in `scripts/interop/README.md`, and verified the same server-role handshake helper shape locally on 2026-04-29. The local helper exited successfully with an advisory after the upstream runner post-check failed, preserving `artifacts/interop-runner/20260429-170106187-server-nginx/` for audit. The first hosted dispatch reached the helper and uploaded a failure bundle, then exposed that Docker-context staging still depended on Windows-only `robocopy`; the helper now stages the pruned build context through PowerShell/.NET file-copy APIs so hosted Ubuntu can reach the Docker build. The second hosted dispatch built the image and uploaded a failure bundle, then exposed missing external runner Python dependencies; the workflow now installs the runner's `requirements.txt` before invoking the helper. The third and fourth hosted dispatches reached the runner and produced JSON/Markdown, then showed that the runner registry compliance preflight can skip selected helper slots before the requested testcase runs; the helper now marks only the explicitly selected slots compliant for that unrelated preflight so the advisory lane reaches the actual handshake testcase. The fifth hosted dispatch reached Docker Compose startup for the selected handshake cell, then exposed that the GitHub-hosted runner's default Docker Engine did not support the upstream runner's `interface_name` compose field; the workflow now installs the latest stable Docker Engine through `docker/setup-docker-action@v5` before invoking the helper. The sixth hosted dispatch reached the runner trace-analysis path, then exposed pyshark/event-loop incompatibility after `setup-python` floated to Python 3.14.4; the workflow now pins Python 3.12 for the external runner dependency set. The seventh hosted dispatch completed the managed server handshake response with a clean quic-go client exit, then failed the runner trace post-check because hosted Ubuntu lacked `editcap` and `tshark`; the workflow now installs `tshark` before invoking the helper. This does not claim broad interop readiness.
